metadata.seed: When people give generic answers to your problems.. Anyone else hate when you're venting to someone about your current struggles and all they say back in response is: "hugs~" or "I don't know what to say, but that sucks" my best friend, who also struggles with mental illness, does it everytime I vent and it annoys me and I don't know why. On one hand I know people's words can't fix you or make everything better, but on the other it'd be nice to at least have someone to talk to (who isn't a therapist) that gets it and actually engages with you from time to time.

patient.query: When people give generic answers to your problems.. Anyone else hate when you're venting to someone about your current struggles and all they say back in response is: "hugs~" or "I don't know what to say, but that sucks" my best friend, who also struggles with mental illness, does it everytime I vent and it annoys me and I don't know why. On one hand I know people's words can't fix you or make everything better, but on the other it'd be nice to at least have someone to talk to (who isn't a therapist) that gets it and actually engages with you from time to time.

triggers: best friend responds with generic phrases
automatic_thoughts: they don't care about my struggles
I need someone to engage with me

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of not coping with treatment (finding)
Tends to be critical of others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're feeling frustrated when people respond with generic phrases, especially from someone you expect to understand, like your best friend. That must be really disappointing when you're looking for more meaningful connection.

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're feeling frustrated when people respond with generic phrases, especially from someone you expect to understand, like your best friend. That must be really disappointing when you're looking for more meaningful connection.

socratic_questioning.response:
FINAL RESPONSE:
<therapist message>
It sounds frustrating when responses feel so generic, especially when you're hoping for genuine connection. How do those kinds of replies typically make you feel?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds really frustrating when you feel like your struggles aren't being truly heard. Have you ever thought about what a close friend who wasn't dealing with mental illness might say in a similar situation?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing
retrieved_concepts_used.label: neutral

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: Tends to be critical of others
retrieved_concepts_used.label: neutral

safety_check.response: I hear how frustrating it can be when people respond with generic phrases, especially when you're sharing something meaningful. When you said that, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?
